name: aoa-routing-advisory-route-authority-boundary
proof_question: Can aoa-routing's two-stage skill routing and owner-layer shortlist hints remain advisory, without becoming skill activation authority, proof authority, or central eval verdict authority?
origin_need: The routing repo exposes generated owner-layer shortlist hints and optional two-stage skill-selection surfaces. These are useful for agent routing, but repeated OS Abyss work depends on them staying advisory rather than becoming activation, proof, scoring, or verdict authority.
summary: Capture routing-local eval pressure for checking that generated route hints and two-stage skill-routing outputs preserve owner boundaries and route to stronger owners for proof, skill meaning, and activation decisions.
object_under_evaluation: aoa-routing generated route hints and two-stage skill selection surfaces
expected_use_when: an agent relies on generated owner-layer shortlist hints to choose an owner repo
an agent uses two-stage skill routing outputs before deciding whether to activate a skill
routing outputs mention aoa-evals, aoa-skills, or other stronger owners and must remain advisory
blind_spot_notes: does not prove the generated router is globally correct
does not decide a central aoa-evals verdict or regression status
does not grant skill activation authority to aoa-routing
does not replace aoa-skills source meaning or central aoa-evals proof doctrine
